From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Traditional districts of Norway](/w/index.php?title=Traditional_districts_of_Norway&redirect=no "Traditional districts of Norway"))

Historical provinces of Norway

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Districts_of_Norway "Special:EditPage/Districts of Norway") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Districts of Norway"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Districts+of+Norway%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Districts+of+Norway%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Districts+of+Norway%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Districts+of+Norway%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Districts+of+Norway%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Districts+of+Norway%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2015)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The country of [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") is historically divided into a number of [districts](/wiki/District "District"). Many districts have deep historical roots, and only partially coincide with today's administrative units of [counties](/wiki/Counties_of_Norway "Counties of Norway") and [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Norway "Municipalities of Norway"). The districts are defined by geographical features, often [valleys](/wiki/Valley "Valley"), [mountain ranges](/wiki/Mountain_range "Mountain range"), [fjords](/wiki/Fjord "Fjord"), plains, or coastlines, or combinations of the above. Many such regions were [petty kingdoms](/wiki/Petty_kingdom "Petty kingdom") up to the early [Viking Age](/wiki/Viking_Age "Viking Age").

## Regional identity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Regional identity")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ac/Kart_over_S%C3%B8r-Noreg.png/250px-Kart_over_S%C3%B8r-Noreg.png)](/wiki/File:Kart_over_S%C3%B8r-Noreg.png)

Southern Norway's districts during the Middle Ages

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/Kart_over_Haalogaland.png/250px-Kart_over_Haalogaland.png)](/wiki/File:Kart_over_Haalogaland.png)

Northern Norway's districts during the Middle Ages

A high percentage of Norwegians identify themselves more by the district they live in or come from, than the formal administrative unit(s) whose jurisdiction they fall under[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. A significant reason for this is that the districts, through their strong geographical limits, have historically delineated the region(s) within which one could travel without too much trouble or expenditure of time and money (on foot or [skis](/wiki/Ski "Ski"), by horse/ox-drawn cart or [sleigh](/wiki/Sleigh "Sleigh") or [dog sled](/wiki/Dog_sled "Dog sled"), or by one's own small [rowing](/wiki/Watercraft_rowing "Watercraft rowing") or sail boat). Thus, [dialects](/wiki/Dialect "Dialect") and regional commonality in [folk culture](/wiki/Folk_culture "Folk culture") tended to correspond to those same geographical units, despite any division into administrative districts by authorities.

In modern times the whole country has become more closely connected, based on the following:

* Communication technologies such as telegraph, newspapers, telephone, radio and TV, in particular [Televerket](/wiki/Telenor "Telenor") and [NRK](/wiki/NRK "NRK").
* The construction of mountain crossings, tunnels through mountains, bridges, undersea tunnels; many of these projects, particularly the larger bridges and the undersea tunnels, were undertaken as late as the 1970s forward.
* Establishing a coastal express route of combined passenger and cargo ships, like the *[Hurtigruten](/wiki/Hurtigruten "Hurtigruten")*, sailing regularly from [Bergen](/wiki/Bergen,_Norway "Bergen, Norway") to [Kirkenes](/wiki/Kirkenes "Kirkenes") and back again, and stopping by at a host of cities and towns along the western and northern coast.
* The construction of [railroads](/wiki/Rail_transport_in_Norway "Rail transport in Norway") between distant parts of the country.
* The opening of dozens of new [airports](/wiki/Category:Airports_in_Norway "Category:Airports in Norway") all over the country through the 1960s and 1970s.
* The release of private cars from government rationing and import restrictions from the 1950s onwards.

A concrete display of the Norwegian habit of identifying themselves by district can be seen in the many regional costumes, called *[bunad](/wiki/Bunad "Bunad")*, strictly connected to distinct districts across the country. Commonly, even city dwellers proudly mark their rural origins by wearing such a costume, from their ancestral landscape, at weddings, visits with members of the royal family, [Constitution Day](/wiki/Norwegian_Constitution_Day "Norwegian Constitution Day") (May 17), and other ceremonial occasions.

## List of traditional districts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: List of traditional districts")]

The following list is non-exhaustive and partially overlapping.

The first name is the name in [Bokmål](/wiki/Bokm%C3%A5l "Bokmål"), the second [Nynorsk](/wiki/Nynorsk "Nynorsk").

### **[Nord-Norge](/wiki/Nord-Norge "Nord-Norge")** / **[Nord-Noreg](/wiki/Nord-Noreg "Nord-Noreg")** (North Norway)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Nord-Norge / Nord-Noreg (North Norway)")]

* [Helgeland](/wiki/Helgeland "Helgeland")
* [Lofoten](/wiki/Lofoten "Lofoten")
* [Ofoten](/wiki/Ofoten "Ofoten")
* [Salten](/wiki/Salten "Salten")
* [Vesterålen](/wiki/Vester%C3%A5len "Vesterålen")

See also [Finnmark](/wiki/Finnmark "Finnmark"), [Hålogaland](/wiki/H%C3%A5logaland "Hålogaland") and [Tromsø](/wiki/Troms%C3%B8 "Tromsø").

### **[Sørlandet](/wiki/S%C3%B8rlandet "Sørlandet")** (Southern Norway)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sørlandet (Southern Norway)")]

* [Agder](/wiki/Agder "Agder")
* [Kristiansandregionen](/wiki/Kristiansand_Region "Kristiansand Region")
* [Lister](/wiki/Vest-Agder "Vest-Agder")
* [Setesdal](/wiki/Setesdal "Setesdal")

### **[Trøndelag](/wiki/Tr%C3%B8ndelag "Trøndelag")**

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Trøndelag")]

* [Fosen](/wiki/Fosen "Fosen")
* [Gauldalen](/wiki/Gauldalen "Gauldalen")
* [Innherad](/wiki/Innherred "Innherred")
* [Namdalen](/wiki/Namdalen "Namdalen")
* [Orkdalen](/wiki/Orkdalen "Orkdalen")
* [Stjørdalen](/wiki/Stj%C3%B8rdalen "Stjørdalen")

### **[Vestlandet](/wiki/Vestlandet "Vestlandet")** (Western Norway)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Vestlandet (Western Norway)")]

* [Dalane](/wiki/Dalane "Dalane")
* [Hardanger](/wiki/Hardanger "Hardanger")
* [Haugalandet](/wiki/Haugalandet "Haugalandet")
* [Jæren](/wiki/J%C3%A6ren "Jæren")
* [Midhordland](/wiki/Midhordland "Midhordland")
* [Nordfjord](/wiki/Nordfjord "Nordfjord")
* [Nordhordland](/wiki/Nordhordland "Nordhordland")
* [Nordmøre](/wiki/Nordm%C3%B8re "Nordmøre")
* [Romsdal](/wiki/Romsdal "Romsdal")
* [Ryfylke](/wiki/Ryfylke "Ryfylke")
* [Sogn](/wiki/Sogn "Sogn")
* [Sunnfjord](/wiki/Sunnfjord "Sunnfjord")
* [Sunnhordland](/wiki/Sunnhordland "Sunnhordland")
* [Sunnmøre](/wiki/Sunnm%C3%B8re "Sunnmøre")
* [Voss](/wiki/Voss_(traditional_district) "Voss (traditional district)")

### **[Østlandet](/wiki/%C3%98stlandet "Østlandet")** / **[Austlandet](/wiki/Austlandet "Austlandet")** (Eastern Norway)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Norway&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Østlandet / Austlandet (Eastern Norway)")]

* [Follo](/wiki/Follo,_Norway "Follo, Norway")
* [Glåmdalen](/wiki/Gl%C3%A5mdal "Glåmdal")
* [Grenland](/wiki/Grenland "Grenland")
* [Gudbrandsdalen](/wiki/Gudbrandsdalen "Gudbrandsdalen")
* [Hadeland](/wiki/Hadeland "Hadeland")
* [Hallingdal](/wiki/Hallingdal "Hallingdal")
* [Hedmarken](/wiki/Hedmarken "Hedmarken")
* [Land](/wiki/Land,_Norway "Land, Norway")
* [Numedal](/wiki/Numedal "Numedal")
* [Ringerike](/wiki/Ringerike_(traditional_district) "Ringerike (traditional district)")
* [Romerike](/wiki/Romerike "Romerike")
* [Toten](/wiki/Toten "Toten")
* [Upper Telemark](/wiki/Upper_Telemark "Upper Telemark")
* [Valdres](/wiki/Valdres "Valdres")
* [Vestfold](/wiki/Vestfold "Vestfold")
* [Østerdalen](/wiki/%C3%98sterdalen "Østerdalen")
* [Østfold](/wiki/%C3%98stfold "Østfold")

See also [Viken](/wiki/Viken,_Norway "Viken, Norway") and [Vingulmark](/wiki/Vingulmark "Vingulmark").
